title: E002: Add tests for Keystone CRD
summary: CC-0012 adds envtest integration tests (CRD installation, valid CR acceptance, CEL rejection, webhook defaulting) and Chainsaw E2E tests (invalid cron, duplicate plugins) for the Keystone CRD. Webhook registration is wired into main.go, and webhook manifests are generated via controller-gen. Implementation is well-structured, follows established patterns, and covers all planned requirements. One blocking issue: the Chainsaw test file uses apiVersion v1alpha1 instead of v1alpha2 as specified by both the plan and the project's chainsaw-config.yaml.

tests_checklist.item: Integration tests exist with //go:build integration tag
SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable(t) called as first statement in every integration test
Valid CR acceptance tested in both host and clusterRef modes
CEL rejection tests cover all 5 constraint violations
Webhook defaulting tests verify zero-value defaults and explicit value preservation
Chainsaw E2E tests cover invalid cron and duplicate plugins
Tests use namespace isolation via GenerateName

code_quality_checklist.item: envtest helper uses runtime.Caller(0) path resolution matching fakeCRDsDirs() pattern
Import cycle avoided via addToScheme/registerWebhooks callbacks
Reuses validKeystone() helper from webhook unit tests
SPDX headers on all new files
Feature ID (CC-0012) referenced in comments and docs
Webhook manifests generated (not hand-written) via controller-gen

security_checklist.item: gosec nolint annotation justified (InsecureSkipVerify for envtest self-signed cert)
No user input handling in new code

architecture_checklist.item: Operator entrypoint via shared bootstrap pattern followed
envtest skip guard pattern followed (re-export from common)
No scope creep (no reconciler tests, no full E2E suite, no SharedScheme changes)
Chainsaw test apiVersion matches project chainsaw-config.yaml

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated test setup code (centralized in setupEnvTest helper)
Minimal changes to existing files (main.go: 3 lines, webhook.go: 2 marker lines)

fail_fast_checklist.item: envtest setup failures use t.Fatalf (immediate stop)
Webhook server readiness checked before tests run

defensive_checklist.item: runtime.Caller(0) panic on failure (consistent with fakeCRDsDirs)
env.Stop() called in all error paths before t.Fatalf

architecture_findings.description: Importing commonenvtest package solely for SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable pulls in transitive dependencies (cert-manager, mariadb-operator, external-secrets, gateway-api) into the keystone go.mod. A future improvement could extract the skip guard into a dependency-free sub-package.
architecture_findings.fix: No action needed now (plan-approved design). Consider extracting SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable into internal/common/testutil/envtest/guard/ in a future cleanup task.

issues_found.description: Chainsaw test uses apiVersion chainsaw.kyverno.io/v1alpha1 but the plan (Task 3.1) and review criteria both specify v1alpha2. The project's chainsaw-config.yaml (line 19) uses v1alpha2. This is an inconsistency that could cause parse errors with Chainsaw v0.3+ if the Test kind also requires v1alpha2. If v1alpha1 is intentionally correct for the Test kind (distinct from the Configuration kind), add a DEVIATION comment explaining why.
issues_found.fix: Change line 8 from 'apiVersion: chainsaw.kyverno.io/v1alpha1' to 'apiVersion: chainsaw.kyverno.io/v1alpha2'. If Chainsaw Test kind only supports v1alpha1, add a comment: '# NOTE: Chainsaw Test kind uses v1alpha1 (distinct from Configuration v1alpha2)' and update the review criteria.

issues_found.description: Documentation states E2E tests use 'the Chainsaw framework (chainsaw.kyverno.io/v1alpha1)' but should reference the same apiVersion used in the actual test file after the C1-1 fix above.
issues_found.fix: Update line 398 to match the corrected apiVersion in chainsaw-test.yaml (v1alpha2 if C1-1 is fixed to v1alpha2, or add the same clarifying note if v1alpha1 is retained with a DEVIATION comment).

suggested_improvements: Consider extracting SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable into a dependency-free sub-package to avoid pulling cert-manager, mariadb-operator, and external-secrets into every operator's go.mod (future cleanup, not blocking).
next_steps: Fix C1-1: Update chainsaw-test.yaml apiVersion to v1alpha2 (or add DEVIATION comment if v1alpha1 is correct for Test kind)
Fix DA3-1: Update docs/reference/keystone-crd.md line 398 to match the corrected apiVersion

detected_patterns.name: Operator-specific envtest helper with webhook server and callback-based scheme registration
detected_patterns.applies_when: Adding envtest integration tests for a new operator that has defaulting/validating webhooks and needs to test through the full admission pipeline (not just unit-testing webhook functions)
detected_patterns.description: Each operator that needs webhook integration testing creates a dedicated envtest setup helper in operators/<name>/internal/testutil/envtest_setup.go. The helper: (1) resolves CRD and webhook manifest paths via runtime.Caller(0) relative to the helper file, (2) starts envtest.Environment with CRDDirectoryPaths and WebhookInstallOptions, (3) builds a local scheme using callbacks (addToScheme, registerWebhooks) to avoid import cycles with the api/v1alpha1 package, (4) creates a controller-runtime manager with a webhook server bound to envtest-allocated host/port/certDir, (5) waits for the webhook server TLS endpoint to accept connections, (6) returns the manager's client. This differs from the common SetupEnvTest (which has no webhook support) and keeps SharedScheme() unmodified. Re-exports SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable from the common envtest package.
